title: rudbeckia grandiflora var. alismifolia
intro: Rudbeckia grandiflora var. alismifolia, also known as Green Coneflower or Yellow Coneflower, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and dark green foliage. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Rudbeckia grandiflora var. alismifolia have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. This plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
